beats.activity: Wake up, feed the dog, quick breakfast and coffee at home.
beats.reasoning: Mia starts her Monday morning at home with full energy; she's an early riser who tends to her pup first thing and fuels up before heading out.

beats.activity: Drive from home to Fishermen's Terminal to check boat and prep for potential fishing work.
beats.reasoning: Mia's primary workplace is the Terminal; she drives across town early to assess weather conditions and vessel readiness for the week—a routine Monday task for a commercial fisherman.

beats.activity: Inspect boat, check nets and equipment, chat with fellow fishermen about the sunny forecast.
beats.reasoning: Perfect weather day motivates an extended prep session; Mia uses this sunny morning to ensure her vessel is ready and network with colleagues about fishing conditions.

beats.activity: Drive downtown to catch the USA vs Wales FIFA match at a bar with close friends.
beats.reasoning: The 11am match is a social event; Mia values time with close friends over crowds, so she's heading to a favorite spot downtown to watch the game and enjoys the early-opening bar scene.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup match at a downtown bar with friends, order food and drinks.
beats.reasoning: Mia attends the 11am match as scheduled; she's generous with money and enjoys the lively atmosphere with her close circle, staying through the full game and early afternoon.

beats.activity: Walk to nearby lunch spot and grab a meal with friends before heading home.
beats.reasoning: After the match, Mia extends the social time with a leisurely lunch before the afternoon; the sunny 74F weather makes outdoor/walkable dining appealing.

beats.activity: Drive home to Bellevue, taking the scenic route to enjoy the sunny day.
beats.reasoning: Mia heads home mid-afternoon; the Light Rail closure (9am–3pm) doesn't affect her since she's driving, and the beautiful weather makes the commute pleasant.

beats.activity: Walk the dog around the neighborhood, enjoy the sunshine and fresh air.
beats.reasoning: Mia makes time for her pup on this beautiful day; a neighborhood walk is her way to decompress after socializing and recharge before evening.

beats.activity: Volunteer shift at local food bank, packing and organizing supplies.
beats.reasoning: Mia prioritizes volunteering as part of her regular routine; a Monday afternoon slot fits her generous nature and desire to give back to the community.
Short commute back to Bellevue after volunteering; Mia is winding down for the evening.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner, eat with the dog nearby, practice salsa dancing in the living room to music.
beats.reasoning: Mia unwinds at home with her favorite hobbies—cooking, chatting with her pup, and dancing—on a full, satisfying day; the sunny weather and social morning leave her in a content mood.

beats.activity: Wind down with tea, journal, and light reading on the couch with the dog.
beats.reasoning: Mia transitions into evening relaxation mode; a sentimental person, she reflects on her day and prepares for bed in a calm, quiet space.

beats.activity: Sleep soundly after a full, balanced day.
beats.reasoning: Mia ends her day with a full 7.5 hours of sleep, having balanced work prep, social time, volunteering, and self-care; she's well-rested for Tuesday.

diary: Woke up early to feed the pup and grab coffee before heading to the terminal. The boats looked good, nets in solid shape—the forecast had everyone buzzing. Ended up ditching the water for the day though; couldn't pass up watching the match with the crew downtown. Pike Place was packed, everyone was into it. We grabbed beers and food, and there's something about that energy that just hits different on a sunny morning. Walked around a bit after, soaked up the weather. Got home and took the dog out, then volunteered at the food bank for a couple hours—felt good to do something useful. Made dinner, danced around the living room like an idiot, and now I'm here with tea and my journal, the dog snoring next to me. It was one of those days where everything just flowed right.
thought_process: I knew the weather was perfect, but honestly the World Cup match was too good to pass up—those moments with friends don't come around every week. The light rail closure didn't affect me since I drove, which worked out fine. I felt torn between the water and the social thing, but my gut said the match would be worth it, and it was. The sunny day had this pull to it; I took the scenic route home just to extend that feeling. By afternoon I needed to balance it out, so the food bank felt right. I've been trying to keep things even—work, play, purpose. The salsa dancing was spontaneous, needed to burn off some energy. Today felt full in a good way, not frantic.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
